Container.Name: signal-cli-gateway
Container.Overview:
Configurable, security-hardened Docker container for the signal-cli daemon. Designed for Hermes Agent integration and other automation use cases.
Supports 4 security modes via SECURITY_MODE env var:
loopback (default) signal-cli daemon on 127.0.0.1:8080, no auth. Requires --network host (loopback inside bridge is unreachable).
loopback-proxy (recommended) signal-cli on 127.0.0.1:8080 + auth proxy on 127.0.0.1:8880. Bearer token + IP allowlist protects the proxy. Requires --network host.
exposed-proxy Proxy binds 0.0.0.0:8880 for remote access. Works on bridge too — add port mapping (-p 8880:8880). signal-cli stays on loopback internally.
unix signal-cli on UNIX socket + socat TCP bridge. Requires --network host.
FIRST USE — Link your phone (one-time): docker run --rm -it \ -v /mnt/user/appdata/signal-cli-gateway:/opt/signal-cli-data \ -e SIGNAL_ACCOUNT=+123****7890 \ ghcr.io/libre-7/signal-cli-gateway:latest \ bash /scripts/link-account.sh
Then scan the QR code from Signal app → Settings → Linked Devices.

Config.Name: SIGNAL_ACCOUNT
Config.Description: Your Signal phone number in E.164 format. REQUIRED.

Config.Name: SECURITY_MODE
Config.Description: Security mode: loopback, loopback-proxy, exposed-proxy, unix.

Config.Name: SECURITY_PROXY_TOKEN
Config.Description: Bearer token for proxy authentication. Auto-generated if empty (logged at startup).

Config.Name: SECURITY_PROXY_ALLOWED_IPS
Config.Description: CIDR ranges that bypass proxy auth. Hermes container IP should be included.

Config.Name: PROXY_PORT
Config.Description: Proxy listener port. External clients connect here.

Config.Name: LOG_LEVEL
Config.Description: Proxy log level: info, debug, warn, error.

Config.Name: Data Volume
Config.Description: Persistent storage for Signal identity keys and config. MUST persist across container updates.
